title: Sour Fluffy Japanese Tofu Bowl
description: A lowcarb, crowdpleasing bowl of airy tofu tossed in a tangy, slightly spicy sauce with a tandooristyle char and fresh herbs.

ingredients.name: Red pepper paste (gochujang or Filipino red pepper paste)

instructions: Press the tofu for 15 minutes to remove excess moisture, then cut into 1‑inch cubes.
In a medium bowl, whisk together red pepper paste, tandoori masala, soy sauce, rice vinegar, lime juice, sesame oil, minced garlic, minced ginger, water, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and pour the sauce into the pan. Bring to a gentle simmer, letting the flavors meld for 2 minutes.
Add the tofu cubes to the simmering sauce, stir gently, and cook for 5 minutes, allowing the tofu to absorb the sauce.
Remove the tofu from the sauce and set aside. Drain any excess liquid from the pan.

Return the skillet to medium‑high heat, add a drizzle of sesame oil, and carefully place the tofu cubes back in the pan, searing each side for 2–3 minutes until a slight char forms—this gives the tandoori finish.
Whisk the remaining sauce, adding a splash more lime or vinegar if desired for extra sourness.
Arrange the tofu in a bowl, drizzle with the warmed sauce, and top with cucumber slices, chopped cilantro, and a pinch of chili flakes.
Serve immediately as a refreshing, low‑carb lunch bowl.
Enjoy the airy texture and tangy kick of this fusion bowl!
